IF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MOVES FOR RELIEF FROM THE ORDER, THE ORDER WILL BE STAYED UNTIL THAT MOTION IS DECIDED. OTHERWISE, A JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE WILL BE ENTERED. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Scott D. Bleifer In Support
Off Calendar Apr-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOSE APLICANO's Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanction

Motion Granted with Order stayed for 30 days. Moving party will serve plaintiff's counsel with a copy of the Order by email and mail or personal delivery with some form of delivery confirmation. Proof of service to be filed. If plaintiff's counsel moves for relief from the Order, the Order will be stayed until that motion is decided. Otherwise, a judgment of dismissal with prejudice will be entered. Prevailing party to prepare a form of Order consistent with the adopted ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to greg@groeneveldlaw.com

Judge Pro Tem: Greg Groeneveld, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
